rulings.Case Title: KUO HSUN CHANG VS. MANUEL GARCIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative For Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, October 27, 2015, line 2. PLAINTIFF KUO CHANG's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative For Summary Adjudication DENIED. (1) Triable issues exist as to Separate Statement of Material Fact Nos. 9 and 18. (2) The complaint pleads damages as an element of its unlawful detainer cause of action and the motion seeks summary judgment on the entire complaint, but the Separate Statement contains no evidence of damages. Northrop, 168 CA 3d 725, is inapposite; it is a commercial case in which damages were sought in a pending parallel action. (3) Plaintiff's cited evidence of bona fide intent to withdraw the property from the rental market is limited to a boilerplate recitation by one of the property's several owners as to his individual state of mind. Sep. Stmt. No. 26; CCP 437c(e). (4) Separate Statement "Issue" Nos. 3-21 fail to state which "facts" supposedly relate to which particular affirmative defenses. CRC 3.1350(b); Weil & Brown, 10:96.5. (5) "Fact 25" does not address the First Affirmative Defense. = (501/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: VLADISLAV CHERNOGUZ VS. YOLANDA CACH ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, October 27, 2015, line 4. DEFENDANTS YOLANDA CACH, HELBERTH BALAM and GERARDO CACH's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT OVERRULED; defendants have five days from the notice of entry of order to answer the complaint. The section of the San Francisco Rent Ordinance on which defendants' demurrer relies - 37.9(a)(1) - does not apply to notices to quit on nuisance grounds. Moreover, even section 37.9(c) - upon which defendants' demurrer perhaps meant to rely - does not require provision of the rent board's telephone number. = (501/RBU)
